2026-04-22T22:05:54.0365543Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0366095Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0366524Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0366896Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0367214Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0367459Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0367630Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0367825Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0368002Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0368675Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0368901Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0369202Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T22:05:54.0369467Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T22:06:46.75439195Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58524,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders class and RequestIdMiddleware behavior in Core README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 685f2f019a (PR #58396) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58396 added several new public APIs to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (released as `1.0.0-beta.23`):\\n\\n- New **`PlatformHeaders`** static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`)\\n- New **`RequestIdMiddleware`** that sets the `x-request-id` response header on every HTTP response (resolved in priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID)\\n- New **`RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** for end-to-end distributed tracing correlation\\n- **Breaking change**: Replaced 6 individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId()`, `AddAgentServerVersion()`, `AddAgentServerLogging()`, `UseAgentServerRequestId()`, `UseAgentServerVersion()`, `UseAgentServerLogging()`) with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair\\n- **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` — users must now use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` static class is a new public API that forms the wire contract between Foundry platform, agent containers, and downstream storage — it is not mentioned anywhere in the README\\n- `RequestIdMiddleware` behavior (`x-request-id` header resolution logic) is not documented in Key Concepts or Examples\\n- The migration from `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName/ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` to `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey/ChatIsolationKey` is not reflected in the README\\n- No code example showing how to use `PlatformHeaders` constants in a custom middleware or handler\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n\\nIn the **Key Concepts** section, add a new subsection for `PlatformHeaders` after the `Health endpoint` entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\nA static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer wire protocol. Use these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings:\\n\\n| Constant | Header name | Direction |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request + Response |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request |\\n\\nThe `x-request-id` response header is set automatically on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (registered by `AgentHostBuilder` and by `AddAgentServerCore()`). The value is resolved in priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → new GUID.\\n```\\n\\nAlso update the **Logging** entry under **Troubleshooting** to mention the migration from the old extension methods:\\n\\nReplace the existing sentence:\\n\\u0026gt; for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it.\\n\\nWith:\\n\\u0026gt; for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it. Note: if upgrading from beta.22, the six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId()`, `AddAgentServerVersion()`, `AddAgentServerLogging()`, and their `Use*` counterparts) have been consolidated into this single pair.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage in a test file at:\\n`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample_PlatformHeaders.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_PlatformHeaders\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        public void UsePlatformHeaderConstants()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders\\n            // Use PlatformHeaders constants instead of hard-coding header name strings.\\n            string requestIdHeader = PlatformHeaders.RequestId;           // \\u0026#34;x-request-id\\u0026#34;\\n            string serverVersionHeader = PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion;   // \\u0026#34;x-platform-server\\u0026#34;\\n            string userIsolationKey = PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey;   // \\u0026#34;x-agent-user-isolation-key\\u0026#34;\\n            string chatIsolationKey = PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey;   // \\u0026#34;x-agent-chat-isolation-key\\u0026#34;\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            Assert.That(requestIdHeader, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-request-id\\u0026#34;));\\n            Assert.That(serverVersionHeader, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-platform-server\\u0026#34;));\\n            Assert.That(userIsolationKey, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-agent-user-isolation-key\\u0026#34;));\\n            Assert.That(chatIsolationKey, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-agent-chat-isolation-key\\u0026#34;));\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd the following snippet placeholder to the README in the **Examples** section (currently it only has a link to samples — add a subsection before the link):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Reading platform header names\\n\\nUse `PlatformHeaders` constants to access header names without hard-coding strings:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already contains all required sections. The gaps are within existing sections:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — missing `PlatformHeaders` subsection (add as described above)\\n2. **Examples** — only has a bare link; should include at least one inline snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage\\n3. **Troubleshooting / Logging** — should note the beta.22 → beta.23 extension method consolidation\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for section formatting.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair\\n- **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` — users must now use `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` static class is a new public API that forms the wire contract between Foundry platform, agent containers, and downstream storage — it is not mentioned anywhere in the README\\n- `RequestIdMiddleware` behavior (`x-request-id` header resolution logic) is not documented in Key Concepts or Examples\\n- The migration from `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName/ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` to `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey/ChatIsolationKey` is not reflected in the README\\n- No code example showing how to use `PlatformHeaders` constants in a custom middleware or handler\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n\\nIn the **Key Concepts** section, add a new subsection for `PlatformHeaders` after the `Health endpoint` entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\nA static class centralizing all platform HTTP header name constants used across the AgentServer wire protocol. Use these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings:\\n\\n| Constant | Header name | Direction |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request + Response |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request |\\n\\nThe `x-request-id` response header is set automatically on every response by `RequestIdMiddleware` (registered by `AgentHostBuilder` and by `AddAgentServerCore()`). The value is resolved in priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` header → new GUID.\\n```\\n\\nAlso update the **Logging** entry under **Troubleshooting** to mention the migration from the old extension methods:\\n\\nReplace the existing sentence:\\n\\u0026gt; for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it.\\n\\nWith:\\n\\u0026gt; for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it. Note: if upgrading from beta.22, the six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId()`, `AddAgentServerVersion()`, `AddAgentServerLogging()`, and their `Use*` counterparts) have been consolidated into this single pair.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage in a test file at:\\n`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample_PlatformHeaders.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_PlatformHeaders\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        public void UsePlatformHeaderConstants()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_PlatformHeaders\\n            // Use PlatformHeaders constants instead of hard-coding header name strings.\\n            string requestIdHeader = PlatformHeaders.RequestId;           // \\u0026#34;x-request-id\\u0026#34;\\n            string serverVersionHeader = PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion;   // \\u0026#34;x-platform-server\\u0026#34;\\n            string userIsolationKey = PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey;   // \\u0026#34;x-agent-user-isolation-key\\u0026#34;\\n            string chatIsolationKey = PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey;   // \\u0026#34;x-agent-chat-isolation-key\\u0026#34;\\n            #endregion\\n\\n#if !SNIPPET\\n            Assert.That(requestIdHeader, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-request-id\\u0026#34;));\\n            Assert.That(serverVersionHeader, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-platform-server\\u0026#34;));\\n            Assert.That(userIsolationKey, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-agent-user-isolation-key\\u0026#34;));\\n            Assert.That(chatIsolationKey, Is.EqualTo(\\u0026#34;x-agent-chat-isolation-key\\u0026#34;));\\n#endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd the following snippet placeholder to the README in the **Examples** section (currently it only has a link to samples — add a subsection before the link):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Reading platform header names\\n\\nUse `PlatformHeaders` constants to access header names without hard-coding strings:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already contains all required sections. The gaps are within existing sections:\\n\\n1. **Key concepts** — missing `PlatformHeaders` subsection (add as described above)\\n2. **Examples** — only has a bare link; should include at least one inline snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage\\n3. **Troubleshooting / Logging** — should note the beta.22 → beta.23 extension method consolidation\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` for section formatting.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T22:08:19.0461553Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T22:07:25.41843984Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":23,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service
